messages.content:
CVE-2021-25022 is a security vulnerability found in the UpdraftPlus WordPress Backup Plugin before version 1.16.66. This issue involves Reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) due to improper sanitization and escaping of the "backup_timestamp" and "job_id" parameters. As a result, attackers may be able to inject malicious script content, which could be executed in the context of admin pages.

The vulnerability falls under CWE-79, which indicates inadequate input neutralization before placing user-controllable data in web output. This presents a potential risk of unauthorized script execution, affecting users with access to the affected pages on WordPress sites. The severity is categorized as MEDIUM, highlighting the importance of timely mitigation efforts.

Systems using UpdraftPlus versions prior to 1.16.66 are vulnerable. Users are advised to update to the latest version to mitigate risks. Additionally, employing cross-site scripting protection mechanisms and regularly assessing web application security can further reduce potential exploitation. The issue is associated with MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1190, emphasizing its relevance in the context of exploiting public-facing applications.
